title: No --mount option with "podman build" nor Containerfile
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

Podman should support the --mount option with the "podman build" command and in the Containerfile using a RUN directive (similar to secrets)

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Build a Containerfile like this:
FROM registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/ubi
RUN --mount=type=image,source=registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest,destination=/mnt,rw=true
RUN yum install -y openssl


2. Option containers/podman#1: Run this command: podman build .


3. Option containers/podman#2: Run this command: podman build --mount=type=image,source=registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest,destination=/mnt,rw=true .


**Describe the results you received:**
Option containers/podman#1 will give the following error: Error: error building at STEP "RUN --mount=type=image,source=registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest,destination=/mnt,rw=true ": error resolving mountpoints for container "0280ebccf2a8f4637e563dabb957e6d73e7313d548b309944dd357f6949d61ed": invalid filesystem type "image"


Option containers/podman#2 will give the following error: Error: unknown flag: --mount


**Describe the results you expected:**
I would expect podman to mount the image source during a build.


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.2.3
API Version:  3.2.3
Go Version:   go1.16.5
Built:        Fri Jul 16 16:33:32 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
KVM machine running Fedora 34 on top of RHEL 8.4

comments.body: @ashley-cui PTAL, weird thing here is that this is going cross platform.  Currently we get --mount support from buildah,  Buildah does not currently understand a mounted image.

I don't think either of these are implemented in buildah? `buildah bud -h` doesn't show a --mount flag, and an image as a RUN mount is supported yet; Docker doesn't have the mount=type=image option in the dockerfile spec either. 

 I'd need more clarification on what this would mean too.

I don't think RUN secrets are wired into podman yet either, only in buildah.

Yes the RUN Secrets will get vendored into Podman as soon as we vendor, which should have just happened podman 3.3

type=image is a podman specific thing.

We have podman image mount ...  Which is what Scott is thinking.

Do we really need to support `type=image` during builds?  The issue states that "Podman should support the --mount option" which it does.  It just doesn't support `type=image`.

I don't think that mounting images like that _during builds_ makes sense since this is already supported via multi-stage builds (e.g., `COPY $PATH FROM $PREVIOUS_STAGE`).
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

@vrothberg  yeah, we need type=image so that we can build on UBI Micro without using multi-stage builds. The multi-stage builds are bad because they overwrite everything in UBI Micro (in the second stage).

If we could mount an image at build, we could execute the YUM/DNF/RPM commands from UBI Standard, and add files to UBI Micro.

@fatherlinux `--mount` option got merged yesterday in https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/3548, we can add support for buildkit like `from`  so final instruction should look like ` --mount=type=bind,from=<imagename>,target=/insidecontainer/image` this should suffice the feature requested in this issue.
@rhatdan PR is not merged yet could we reopen please.

@flouthoc @rhatdan I looked in the podman-run page, and the syntax appears to be:

--mount=type=image,source=fedora,destination=/fedora-image,rw=true

Is the above what we think will work after this PR gets merged (maybe already merged?). I'm actually trying to do something a little more tricky, like to following, in a Containerfile:

FROM registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/ubi-micro
RUN --mount=type=image,source=registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/ubi,destination=/mnt /mnt/usr/bin/yum install -y httpd

@fatherlinux PR is not merged yet but after merge it will add support for below syntax. Syntax is similar to `docker/buildkit`.

```dockerfile
FROM registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/ubi-micro
RUN --mount=type=bind,source=.,from=registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/ubi,target=/mnt /mnt/usr/bin/yum install -y httpd
```
@nalind wants some more tests and then it can get merged.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

> @fatherlinux PR is not merged yet but after merge it will add support for below syntax. Syntax is similar to `docker/buildkit`.
> 
> ```dockerfile
> FROM registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/ubi-micro
> RUN --mount=type=bind,source=.,from=registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/ubi,target=/mnt /mnt/usr/bin/yum install -y httpd
> ```

Any movement on the tests? I'd love to see this land!
Some new test requests are in. I'll wrap them up by this weekend.

This is such cool work! Thank you for doing it!
